description:
You are a machine-learning engineer who is preparing training-dataset artifacts.  
    A mock “API” has already been staged for you and is reachable through the local
    file-URL below.  
    Your goal is to copy the JSON payload that it returns into your own working
    directory *using a single curl invocation* and then record a one-line success
    log.

    Required steps (all of them must be satisfied; the automated tests are exact):

    1. Create the directory  
          /home/user/prep  
       if it does not already exist.

    2. Use curl to download the exact payload located at  
          file:///home/user/api_source/dataset_schema.json  
       and save it (byte-for-byte identical) to  
          /home/user/prep/dataset_schema.json

       • The destination file must be named **dataset_schema.json**.  
       • The JSON file must be **identical** to the source: every
         character, whitespace, and newline must match.  
       • Use curl’s –o/–output switch rather than stdout redirection, so that
         the command remains a single curl operation.

    3. Immediately after the curl operation completes, create a text file  
          /home/user/prep/curl_log.txt  
       that contains exactly one line (no leading/trailing blank lines):

          SUCCESS: dataset_schema.json downloaded

       The line must end with a single Unix newline (LF, “\n”).

    4. You may combine everything (directory creation, curl call, and log
       creation) on a single command line by chaining shell operators, but
       the only network/file-transfer tool you are allowed to use is
       **curl**.

    Verification details the grader will check:

       • /home/user/prep/dataset_schema.json exists.  
       • Its contents are byte-for-byte identical to the source JSON.  
       • /home/user/prep/curl_log.txt exists and contains exactly the line
         shown above followed by a single newline.  

    Do not print anything else to stdout except what your command naturally
    produces (curl progress or status is acceptable).  No human explanation
    is required—only execute the terminal command(s) so that the two files
    are created in their required state.
truth:
<!--
    Pre-existing artefacts before the agent starts.
    These MUST NOT appear in the public <task> description.
    -->
    <initial_state>
        <!-- Directory and file that simulate the “API” -->
        /home/user/api_source/             (0775)
        /home/user/api_source/dataset_schema.json (0644)
    </initial_state>

    <initial_file_content path="/home/user/api_source/dataset_schema.json"><![CDATA[
{
  "dataset": "iris",
  "version": "1.0",
  "features": ["sepal_length", "sepal_width", "petal_length", "petal_width"],
  "target": "species",
  "classes": ["setosa", "versicolor", "virginica"]
}
]]></initial_file_content>

    <expected_final_state>
        /home/user/prep/                         (directory must exist, any reasonable mode)
        /home/user/prep/dataset_schema.json      (must match byte-for-byte the initial JSON)
        /home/user/prep/curl_log.txt             (0644, single line described below)
    </expected_final_state>

    <log_expected_contents path="/home/user/prep/curl_log.txt"><![CDATA[
SUCCESS: dataset_schema.json downloaded
]]></log_expected_contents>
